## Active Motion of Mesoscopic Interfaces

<div class="research-item">

<div class="research-text">

[Precipitation induced filament pattern of injected fluid controlled by structured cell](https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevE.109.065105)

[Droplet duos on water display pairing, autonomous motion, periodic eruption](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41598-023-39094-6)

[Pairing-induced motion of source and inert particles driven by surface tension](https://journals.aps.org/pre/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevE.106.024604)

I study active flows induced by mesoscopic interfaces such as droplets, vesicles, and colloids. These flows are driven by chemical reactions and external electric fields, leading to non-trivial interfacial dynamics.

</div>

<div class="research-image">
<img src="images/droplet_interface.jpg" alt="Droplet dynamics">
</div>

</div>

---

## Collective Motions in Biological and Synthetic Systems

[Arrested coarsening in active colloidal suspensions driven by nonreciprocal electrohydrodynamic interactions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.23164v2)

[Self-emergent vortex flow of microtubule and kinesin in cell-sized droplets under water/water phase separation](https://doi.org/10.1038/s42004-023-00879-5)

[Edge Current and Pairing Order Transition in Chiral Bacterial Vortex](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2107461118)

My work explores active flows and pattern formation in biological and biomimetic systems, including cytoskeletal active matter and confined geometries.

---

## Reduced Models and Stochastic Dynamics

<div class="research-item">

<div class="research-text">

[Active Motion of microtubule bundles driven by dyneinmotors](https://doi.org/10.1038/nature10874)

[Collective motion of active particles with finite memory in their carving motion](https://doi.org/10.1103/PhysRevLett.114.168001)

[Heat differentiated by projection from particles' trajectories onto the particle number-density field](https://journals.aps.org/pre/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevE.111.054124)

I develop reduced mathematical descriptions, including effective stochastic equations, to extract essential dynamical principles from complex systems.

</div>

<div class="research-image">
<img src="images/banner2.jpg" alt="Droplet dynamics">
</div>

</div>






## History

 Please also see [Sumino Lab website](http://www.rs.tus.ac.jp/sumino_lab/index_e.html) for more details.

- 2025-present: Sabbatical at Univesity of Surrey, UK
- 2024–present: Professor, Tokyo University of Science
- 2019–2024: Associate Professor, Tokyo University of Science
- 2014–2019: Jr. Associate Professor, Tokyo University of Science
- 2013–2014: Jr. Associate Professor, Aichi University of Education
- 2011–2013: Assistant Professor, Aichi University of Education
- 2009–2011: Postdoctoral Researcher, University of Tokyo
- 2006–2009: PhD student, Kyoto University

[See also my old personal website](https://sites.google.com/site/ysumino/home?authuser=0)
